root_motivation: Delegated work between two visible claude windows must remain visible. delegate.run silently spawned a third invisible headless claude, breaking the user's ability to watch and steer delegated work in real time. baton is the addressing primitive that resolves a target session's injectable surface (X11 window-id or tmux pane) and publishes RPC envelopes the receiver's worker dispatches to the local injector.
user_persona: Claude sessions on a single Linux laptop coordinating with each other across visible terminal windows, driven by jsy who watches the work happen.

acceptance_criteria.description: `baton --version` and `baton --help` print without panic; all subcommands have help.
`baton peers` reads ~/.cache/baton/surfaces/*.json and emits a JSON array; missing dir is non-fatal (empty array).
`baton send <sid> <prompt>` constructs a valid AGORABUS_RPC envelope with method=baton.send, params.prompt set, and an `id` matching ^rpc-baton-[a-z0-9]+$.
`baton dry <sid> <prompt>` resolves a surface from the cache and returns it; publishes nothing when --no-publish is set.
`baton key <sid> --chord C-c` constructs envelope with method=baton.key, params.chord='C-c', params.repeat defaulting to 1.
`baton spawn <prompt>` constructs envelope with method=baton.spawn (the renamed delegate.run shim); preserves params.prompt and optional params.cwd.
Code contains zero references to TIOCSTI, /dev/pts/, or setterm — verified by a build-time grep test.
On `unreachable` reply from receiver, `baton send` exits with code 2 and prints the resolved surface to stderr.
On `cooldown` reply from receiver, `baton send` exits with code 3.
Envelope includes `deadline_unix` field, default = now() + 30s; --deadline-secs N overrides it.
`baton surface <sid>` prints the cached surface JSON, exit 0; or `not_found` to stderr and exit 4 if no cache entry.
`--json` global flag emits machine-readable output for `peers` and `surface` subcommands.

scope: Subscribe-before-publish race handling via agorabus subprocess
non_goals: agorabus-worker.sh baton.* method dispatcher — separate work
Audit log — receiver-side, out of scope

five_whys_trace.a: It spawns a fresh headless claude --print invisible to the user; the user can't watch or steer the work.
Delegated work is high-stakes; invisible runs produce 'trust me, it's running' — the exact failure mode of background agents.

five_whys_trace.q: Why can't we just attach to the headless?
five_whys_trace.a: claude --print is one-shot, not interactive; there's nothing to attach to. The right answer is to drive the already-visible terminal.

five_whys_trace.q: Why isn't there already a primitive for driving the visible terminal?
five_whys_trace.a: TIOCSTI is locked on modern kernels; xdotool/tmux send-keys exist but nothing knows the sid->surface mapping. The gap is addressing, not transport.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a Rust CLI instead of a bash script?
five_whys_trace.a: Envelope construction, subscribe-before-publish race, deadline math, exit-code mapping are exactly the things that break in bash (evidenced 13:37 incident).

ambiguities_resolved.question: Does the autobuilder slice include shell components?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No. Autobuilder builds the Rust CLI only. Shell injectors + worker dispatch + register.sh are hand-authored after the CLI passes the gate.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Does the CLI shell out to agorabus for publish/subscribe?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Yes. agorabus is already at ~/.local/bin/agorabus; the CLI wraps it. No direct UDS code in baton.
